commit.message:
refactor(api): move webhooks to unified /api/webhooks/{service} path

Move Paddle and Sentry webhook handlers out of versioned API routes
into a dedicated /api/webhooks/ directory. Webhooks are inbound event
receivers from external services with their own auth (HMAC signatures),
not part of the versioned API contract consumed by frontends.

Old paths:
  - POST /api/v1/payments/paddle/webhook
  - POST /api/v1/webhooks/sentry

New paths:
  - POST /api/webhooks/paddle
  - POST /api/webhooks/sentry

All 14 Paddle processor files moved with the handler.
Updated API docs and README to reflect new paths.

BREAKING: Paddle dashboard webhook URL must be updated.

Fix missing static web assets in NuGet package (#118)

The Razor SDK generates the static web assets manifest at dotnet build
time. Previously, npm run build (which outputs the JS files to wwwroot/)
ran after dotnet build, so the manifest was generated with an empty
wwwroot/ and dotnet pack --no-build reused that stale manifest.

Fix by moving the npm install/lint/build steps before dotnet restore and
dotnet build, ensuring the compiled JS files exist in wwwroot/ when the
static web assets manifest is generated.

* feat(ol-superset): add apply-rls command for governance RLS policy management

Add a new `apply-rls` CLI command that applies OL governance row-level
security (RLS) policies to a Superset instance using OAuth PKCE
authentication (no shared admin credentials required).

New files:
- ol_superset/commands/apply_rls.py: cyclopts command with dry-run
  support, production confirmation prompt, and rich table output
- policies/ol_rls_policies.json: canonical RLS filter definitions
  (moved from ol-infrastructure; now co-located with the CLI)

Updated files:
- lib/superset_api.py: four new RLS API helpers (list_roles,
  list_datasets_name_to_id, list_rls_filters, apply_rls_filter) with
  collision detection for ambiguous bare table names
- cli.py: register apply-rls command and add workflow example

This replaces the K8s Job approach (which required an admin password
stored in Vault that was never populated) with an operator-driven
workflow that uses each user's own Keycloak credentials.

Usage:
  ol-superset apply-rls superset-production      # with confirmation
  ol-superset apply-rls superset-qa --yes        # skip prompt
  ol-superset apply-rls superset-production --dry-run

commit.message:
R3 AUDIT: Final MLOps hardening — 3 critical fixes

1. HotSwapLstmModel bidirectional architecture alignment
   - Cloud training scripts (lstm_training.py, DigitalOcean) train bidirectional
     LSTM with FC(256->64->32->3), but HotSwapLstmModel was unidirectional with
     Linear(128->3). TorchSharp load() silently dropped reverse LSTM weights
     and hit FC shape mismatch, producing garbage predictions after cloud deploy.

2. Shadow variant excluded at 0% traffic weight (AWS SageMaker)
   - UpdateEndpointWithTrafficShiftAsync skipped adding new-candidate variant
     when weight=0, but DeployWithTrafficShiftAsync immediately invoked it via
     TargetVariant header for shadow testing. SageMaker returned ValidationError,
     failing the entire A/B deployment pipeline.

3. Online-to-Batch transition state reset
   - After full retrain, OnlineLearningService continued with decayed LR (1e-6),
     stale drift baseline, and pre-retrain replay samples. Added ResetForRetrain()
     and wired it into SmartTrainingService finalization to restore full learning
     capacity after batch retrains.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make setLogFile and openStepLogFile async to prevent stream races

Previously both methods called this._stream.end() synchronously and
immediately created a new stream, meaning the old stream might still be
draining when the new one opened. If the previous stream had unflushed
writes they could be lost or interleaved with the new stream's writes.

Changes:
- setLogFile(filePath): void  →  async setLogFile(filePath): Promise<void>
  Uses fs.promises.mkdir and awaits closeLogFile() before opening the
  new write stream, ensuring the old stream is fully drained first.
- openStepLogFile(filePath): void  →  async openStepLogFile(filePath): Promise<void>
  Same pattern: awaits closeStepLogFile() when replacing an open stream.

Update all test call sites to await setLogFile() and openStepLogFile()
so tests exercise the correct async sequencing.

Addresses step_18 code_quality_analyst finding: 'async close methods
use await Promise but open methods are sync, risking silent failures and
race conditions if called in rapid succession.'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

R3-DATA: Final hardening — missing column migrations, stale index cleanup, schema-aware health check

Fix 1: Add AddColumnIfNotExistsAsync for PerformanceReports.SortinoRatio and
Expectancy columns. Entity declared them but DDL migration was missing — existing
databases would throw SqliteException on any read/write to these columns.

Fix 2: Drop stale standalone boolean indexes IX_AssetPerformance_IsEnabled and
IX_Assets_IsActive from DDL path. R2-PASS2 removed them from OnModelCreating
(boolean columns have ~50% selectivity = pure write amplification) but the DDL
CREATE TABLE still created them for databases initialized via EnsureTablesExistAsync.
Also removed the CREATE INDEX lines from the DDL definitions to prevent re-creation.

Fix 3: Upgrade DatabaseHealthCheck from connection-only to schema-aware. Now queries
SchemaVersions table to verify tables are queryable and schema version matches
expected. Reports Degraded if schema is outdated or missing, giving operators
visibility before trading starts.

Schema version bumped from 6.0 to 6.1.

commit.message:
feat: embed virtiofsd as in-process library (#9)

* build: add virtiofsd as optional library dependency

Add virtiofsd v1.13 as an optional, Linux-only dependency behind the
'embedded-virtiofsd' feature flag. This enables in-process virtiofsd
without affecting the default build or macOS compilation.

The feature is opt-in: cargo build --features embedded-virtiofsd

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: in-process virtiofsd backend with integration test

Add virtfs.rs module with VirtiofsBackend that runs virtiofsd as a
thread in the shim process instead of spawning a separate daemon.

- Uses virtiofsd crate's library API (PassthroughFs + VhostUserDaemon)
- Produces the same vhost-user socket that CH connects to
- Behind 'embedded-virtiofsd' feature flag (opt-in)
- Requires libseccomp-dev and libcap-ng-dev on Linux

Integration test proves the full pipeline:
  In-process virtiofsd (277us startup) -> VM boot (167ms)
  -> agent healthy -> shared directory works

Startup improvement: 277us vs ~10ms for child process spawn.
Memory savings: ~5MB RSS per VM (virtiofsd process eliminated).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: wire embedded virtiofsd into VmManager

When the 'embedded-virtiofsd' feature is enabled, VmManager::spawn_virtiofsd()
starts the vhost-user-fs backend as an in-process thread instead of spawning
a child process. The change is transparent — all 18 integration tests pass
with the embedded backend.

Changes:
- VmManager gains optional virtiofsd_backend field (cfg-gated)
- spawn_virtiofsd() dispatches to in-process or child based on feature
- from_restored() initializes the new field
- Unused import cleanup in virtfs.rs

All 18 integration tests pass with --features embedded-virtiofsd:
  test_vm_boot_and_agent_health, test_echo_container_with_networking,
  test_embedded_virtiofsd, test_snapshot_and_restore, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: remove dead code (6 methods, 2 unused types)

Remove methods and types that are never called from production code
or integration tests:

- vm.rs: remove_disk(), add_net(), send_migration(), receive_migration()
- pool.rs: refill(), snapshot_manager()
- types.rs: MigrationConfig, SnapshotConfig

The remaining #[allow(dead_code)] annotations are needed because main.rs
re-declares modules (mod vm;) creating a separate compilation unit where
pub methods used only by integration tests appear unused to the compiler.

commit.message:
feat: add Rust XPIA subprocess bridge (#2992)

* feat: add Rust XPIA subprocess bridge (rust_xpia.py)

Add Python wrapper that calls the amplihack-xpia-defender Rust CLI
binary via subprocess for XPIA validation. NO FALLBACKS.

New files:
- src/amplihack/security/rust_xpia.py: subprocess bridge with functions:
  - validate_content() - scan text for injection attacks
  - validate_bash_command() - validate bash commands
  - validate_webfetch_request() - validate URL+prompt
  - validate_agent_communication() - validate agent messages
  - health_check() - run XPIA health check
  - list_patterns() / get_config() / is_available()
  - find_binary() - locate xpia-defend on PATH or ~/.cargo/bin

- tests/test_rust_xpia.py: 23 integration tests (call actual binary)

Design principles:
- Fail-closed: any subprocess error → RustValidationResult.blocked()
- NO FALLBACKS: binary not found → RustXPIAError (never falls back to Python)
- Content sent via stdin to avoid shell injection via args
- JSON protocol: parse stdout, check exit code (0=valid, 1=blocked, 2=error)

Refs: #2991

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [skip ci] chore: Auto-bump patch version

Tests exercise the FULL production chain in subprocess isolation:

1. TestBinaryDirect (7 tests): xpia-defend CLI binary invoked directly
2. TestPreToolUseHook (7 tests): pre_tool_use.py hook with real Claude
   Code JSON protocol (stdin → JSON stdout → allow/deny)
3. TestSessionStartHook (1 test): session_start.py health check
4. TestRustBridgeFullChain (8 tests): Python → rust_xpia.py → binary
   for all validation types (content, bash, webfetch, agent, health)
5. TestAdversarialAttacks (9 tests): real attack payloads with
   parametrized expected outcomes matching Python parity
6. TestFailClosed (3 tests): binary missing → blocked, garbage output
   → blocked, exit code 2 → blocked (even if JSON says valid)

All 35 tests pass. Verified Python parity for all 'allowed' results.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct Claude Code hook input format (top-level tool_name/tool_input)

The Claude Code PreToolUse hook protocol sends JSON with top-level keys:
  tool_name, tool_input, session_id, cwd, hook_event_name, tool_use_id

Both pre_tool_use.py and the new pre_tool_use_rust.py were incorrectly
parsing nested toolUse.name / toolUse.input, meaning the hooks never
found the tool name and silently allowed all commands.

Changes:
- Fix pre_tool_use.py to parse top-level tool_name/tool_input
- Add pre_tool_use_rust.py: Rust-backed hook via xpia-defend binary
  - Uses cwd from hook input for project root detection
  - Fail-closed: import errors, missing binary, or exceptions → deny
  - Logs to ~/.claude/logs/xpia/rust_security_YYYYMMDD.log
- Update 7 hook protocol tests to use correct input format

Verified in real Claude Code sessions:
- Safe commands (echo, wc -w) → ALLOW with risk=none
- Injection content → DENY with risk=critical, threats=2
- Security log entries written with session_id

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* security: harden xpia-defend installer after code review

Fixes 5 security issues found in code review:

1. CRITICAL: Added SHA256 checksum verification — downloads SHA256SUMS.txt
   from release, verifies archive hash before extraction. Tampered binaries
   are now detected and rejected.

2. CRITICAL: Added path traversal protection for zip extraction — validates
   member names contain no '..' or absolute paths, resolves final path to
   confirm it stays within target directory.

3. HIGH: Fixed file permissions — set explicit 0o755 instead of OR-ing
   execute bits onto existing permissions (which could result in 0o777).

4. HIGH: Improved launcher error handling — separate ImportError from
   runtime errors, log at ERROR level, print clear message that security
   validation will block tool use until binary is available.

5. MEDIUM: Changed find_binary() default to auto_install=False — runtime
   validation calls no longer trigger unexpected network activity. Only
   explicit startup call (copilot.py) passes auto_install=True.

Also: removed unused imports (os, stat), narrowed bare except to specific
exception types, added 8 new tests (27 total).

commit.message:
feat(carto): generate all available coloring variants automatically

Instead of choosing a single coloring (lat/bipolar/unipolar) upfront,
the pipeline now produces all colorings that have valid data in one run.
Subdivision and xatlas UV unwrap are done once per mesh; only the cheap
vertex-color mapping is repeated per coloring (~5% overhead).

- CartoConfig.coloring -> colorings (list of schemes)
- Remove coloring prompt from both single and batch wizards
- --coloring CLI flag becomes a filter (default: all)
